title: rubus trifidus
intro: Rubus trifidus, also known as the Thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its edible white or pink flowers and red or purple fruit. The Thimbleberry has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Thimbleberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
